However, in breast cancer cells Bcl-2 was more efficient than Bcl-xL in activating apoptosis, with different effects on cell cycle regulation, and in blocking cell proliferation without affecting cell viability. The molecular basis of carbapenem and macrolide resistance have not been explained. In the current state of the art of printing technologies, a single-pass (continuous stream) printing technology is still the most commonly employed printing technology. Compared to the other technologies, the most notable feature of the single-pass printing is that on the one hand all the ink dots are deposited in one pass, with continuous supply of ink and without any intermediate drying; and on the other hand all the ink dots are deposited to the same area on the paper.
However, the single-pass printing technology has limitations when the ink is to be used at high temperatures, such as when the printing is carried out by applying the ink as a hot melt. In fact, if the heated ink reaches the paper in one pass, the ink may solidify on the paper, causing the quality of the printing to be degraded.
In order to solve these problems, the present invention proposes a multi-print technology, which allows for an increase in the number of passes required for printing the same image to be performed without compromising the quality of the image, with the advantages of high flexibility of use and low costs.

A new research initiative has been launched to improve how we detect and detect early prostate cancer.

In the past, radiologists would look at a slice of prostate tissue and determine whether there was a suspicious lesion — a tumour, node, or cyst. They then send these images to a pathologist to confirm the diagnosis.

But, according to the study, that pathologist could often identify only 40 percent of the patients in the study, and the rest were deemed to not have prostate cancer.

“I really feel like this is going to change the way we look at prostate cancer, and maybe even the way we treat it,” said Dr. Paul J. Lipschutz, who heads radiation oncology at the University of California, San Francisco’s Norris Comprehensive Cancer Center and was not involved with the study.

“This is the first report to show the possibility of early stage prostate cancer detection by a device that’s able to detect tumour blood vessels, which have been previously shown to be indicators of aggressive prostate cancer,” said Dr. Richard Brabenec of the University of Oxford, who was not involved with the work.

I am using  Java
Here what I want to do is the below is just an example i don't have to do this but i am just illustrating it here

Get the first list of documents by using mongo query
Get the second list of documents by using mongo query

A:

You can execute bulk operations with the aggregation framework:

First, use $unwind, $replace and $sort to get the documents you want to process. You can then use $group to group by some key.
You can then update each document one at a time using map/reduce/filter.

As the value and use of information continue to increase, individuals and businesses seek additional ways to process and store information. One option is an information handling system. An information handling system generally processes, compiles, stores, and/or communicates information or data for business, personal, or other purposes. Because technology and information handling needs and requirements may vary between different applications, information handling systems may also vary regarding what information is handled, how the information is handled, how much information is processed, stored, or communicated, and how quickly and efficiently the information may be processed, stored, or communicated. The variations in information handling systems allow for information handling systems to be general or configured for a specific user or specific use such as financial transaction processing, airline reservations, enterprise data storage, or global communications. In addition, information handling systems may include a variety of hardware and software components that may be configured to process, store, and communicate information and may include one or more computer systems, data storage systems, and networking systems.

Kathleen Pugh is an American actress, playwright and novelist. She writes plays in a style she describes as "non-traditional" and "intriguing without being boring" under the name Katharine Daley.

Career
Pugh was born in Detroit, Michigan, and raised in a strict Catholic environment as the second of four children. As a teenager she was involved in theater at the school run by Sister Margaret Mary from her hometown. She studied acting at the University of Michigan with Mary Beth Williams and worked as an actress while attending college. Pugh began to write plays and scripts while she was a student, but only produced two plays: On the Bended Knee (2006) and The Devil and God: A Dialogue (2008), with performances at the Bush Theatre in Cambridge, Massachusetts, in 2006 and 2008 respectively.

In 2001, Pugh co-wrote the original story for The Devil and God with her partner David Zeltz, then directed and starred in the play, which also starred John Malkow in the title role and David Zeltz in the lead role. Zeltz, Pugh and a friend worked together for ten years to perfect the script for the play.

Whereas the old MPEG‐2 standard was a multistage encoding method, with eight levels (and no looping), the H.264 method is a single‐cycle method that has only two levels. Compared to the previous single‐cycle MPEG‐2 standards, H.264 makes use of a variable‐size GOP or Group Of Pictures (GOP) structure. Furthermore, H.264 can also be seen as a successor of the MPEG transport stream format to the multimedia system, in which data representing different components of a video sequence would be transmitted together.

In contrast to the previous MPEG‐2 standards, H.264 is a variable bit rate (VBR) video coding standard, which implies that each video component of the sequence can be treated differently in terms of its coding behavior, according to its particular content. Rocambarus ameridensis--reanalysis demonstrates two sympatrolong and heteromorphic chromosomes in a New World snake genus.
Rocambarus ameridensis is a New World species of viper with two known species; one has a widespread distribution in South America, the other is restricted to the New World tropics. While species of both genus Rocambo (Loxosceles, Typhlodes) are known to possess metacentric and acrocentrics chromosomes with homologues in different chromosome regions, this is the second report of the karyotype of R. ameridensis. In all species in which karyotype analysis was attempted, the long arms of the two chromosomes were present as a supernumerary chromosome. The karyotype of R. ameridensis was established with the aid of R-banding and Ag-NOR staining. We conclude that these findings represent a new member of the family Typhlodidae.

ROME--Italian doctors said some 566 cases, which ranged in type (cancer and benign tumors) and severity (malignant and benign) in a single year, were linked to a widespread belief that consuming garlic is a cure for cancer.

The study, published in the Lancet medical journal, involved a team of doctors from eight Italian hospitals that had examined 2,250 patients. Of these, 60 cases were judged to be suspicious for cancer, compared to the average of 14.

Garlic itself is not a cure, but doctors say garlic's high concentration of sulfur compounds is good for the body because of its antimicrobial, antifungal, and anti-viral properties.

But some doctors say the patients who ate a lot of garlic were more likely to have a diagnosis of cancer, though this may have been due mainly to faulty diagnoses, the study noted.

"If there is no relation between consumption of garlic and the risk of cancer, the presence of garlic is clearly not related to diagnosis, at least of certain tumors," the authors wrote in their report, "Garlic, Anticancer, Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties of Garlic, with Special Reference to Cervical Cancer.

Autism is a neurodevelopmental disorder characterized by difficulties with social interaction and communication, and by restricted and repetitive behavior. Parents often notice signs during the first three years of their child's life. These signs often develop gradually, though some autistic children experience regression in their communication and social skills after reaching developmental milestones at a normal pace.

Autism is associated with a combination of genetic and environmental factors. Risk factors during pregnancy include certain infections, such as rubella, toxins including valproic acid, alcohol, cocaine, pesticides, lead, and air pollution, fetal growth restriction, and autoimmune diseases. It is similar in shape to the Ancient Greek letter alpha, from which it derives. The uppercase version consists of the two slanting sides of a triangle, crossed in the middle by a horizontal bar. International Atomic Time (TAI, from the French name ) is a high-precision atomic coordinate time standard based on the notional passage of proper time on Earth's geoid. It is a continuous scale of time, without leap seconds. It is the principal realisation of Terrestrial Time (with a fixed offset of epoch). It is also the basis for Coordinated Universal Time (UTC), which is used for civil timekeeping all over the Earth's surface. UTC deviates from TAI by a number of whole seconds. , when another leap second was put into effect, UTC is currently exactly 37 seconds behind TAI. The 37 seconds result from the initial difference of 10 seconds at the start of 1972, plus 27 leap seconds in UTC since 1972.

A related but distinct subject is physical cosmology, which studies the Universe as a whole.

ASCII ( ), abbreviated from American Standard Code for Information Interchange, is a character encoding standard for electronic communication. ASCII codes represent text in computers, telecommunications equipment, and other devices. Most modern character-encoding schemes are based on ASCII, although they support many additional characters.

The Internet Assigned Numbers Authority (IANA) prefers the name US-ASCII for this character encoding.

ASCII is one of the IEEE milestones.

Animation is a method in which figures are manipulated to appear as moving images. In traditional animation, images are drawn or painted by hand on transparent celluloid sheets to be photographed and exhibited on film. Today, most animations are made with computer-generated imagery (CGI). Computer animation can be very detailed 3D animation, while 2D computer animation (which may have the look of traditional animation) can be used for stylistic reasons, low bandwidth, or faster real-time renderings. Other common animation methods apply a stop motion technique to two- and three-dimensional objects like paper cutouts, puppets, or clay figures.

Apollo is one of the  Olympian deities in classical Greek and Roman religion and Greek and Roman mythology. The national divinity of the Greeks, Apollo has been recognized as a god of archery, music and dance, truth and prophecy, healing and diseases, the Sun and light, poetry, and more. One of the most important and complex of the Greek gods, he is the son of Zeus and Leto, and the twin brother of Artemis, goddess of the hunt. Seen as the most beautiful god and the ideal of the kouros (ephebe, or a beardless, athletic youth), Apollo is considered to be the most Greek of all the gods. Apollo is known in Greek-influenced Etruscan mythology as Apulu.

Andre Kirk Agassi ( ; born April 29, 1970) is an American former world No. 1 tennis player. He is an eight-time major champion and a 1996 Olympic gold medalist, as well as a runner-up in seven other Grand Slam tournaments.

Agassi was the first man to win four Australian Open singles titles in the Open Era (though later surpassed by Novak Djokovic, who won his fifth title in 2015 and has since won the tournament nine times). Agassi is the second of five men to achieve the career Grand Slam in the Open Era and the fifth of eight overall to make the achievement. He is also the first of two men to achieve the career Golden Slam (career Grand Slam and Olympic gold medal), and the only man to win a career Super Slam (career Grand Slam, plus the Olympic gold medal and the year-end championships)

The Austroasiatic languages , also known as Mon–Khmer , are a large language family in  Mainland Southeast Asia and South Asia. These languages are scattered throughout parts of Thailand, India, Bangladesh, Nepal, and southern China. There are around 117 million speakers of Austroasiatic languages. Of these languages, only Vietnamese, Khmer, and Mon have a long-established recorded history. Only two have official status as modern national languages: Vietnamese in Vietnam and Khmer in Cambodia.  The Mon language is a recognized indigenous language in Myanmar and Thailand. In Myanmar, the Wa language is the de facto official language of Wa State. Santali is one of the 22 scheduled languages of India. The rest of the languages are spoken by minority groups and have no official status.

Afroasiatic (Afro-Asiatic), also known as Afrasian or Hamito-Semitic, Semito-Hamitic, or Erythraean, is a large language family of about 300 languages that are spoken predominantly in Western Asia, North Africa, the Horn of Africa and parts of the Sahel. With the exception of Semitic, all branches of the Afroаsiatic family are spoken exclusively on the African continent.

Afroasiatic languages have over 500 million native speakers, which is the fourth largest number of native speakers of any language family (after Indo-European, Sino-Tibetan and Niger–Congo).
